title: ActiveMQ Network of Brokers Topology and Bridge Events
dataSources: `index=messaging` `sourcetype=activemq:log` broker logs for `NetworkConnector`, duplex bridges, transport start/stop, and `Discovery` events
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="activemq:log" earliest=-7d
| search ("NetworkConnector" OR "network bridge" OR "duplex" OR "DiscoveryNetworkConnector" OR "Started network bridge" OR "Stopped network bridge" OR "bridge to" OR "Establishing network connection")
| rex field=_raw "NetworkConnector\s+(?<connector>\S+)"
| stats count as events latest(_raw) as sample by host, connector
| sort -events
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: In a network of brokers, bridge connections carry cross-site traffic; unexpected stops or flapping `NetworkConnector` lines correlate with split-brain message routing and duplicate delivery risk. Structured alerting on these log signatures supports DR drills and firewall change validation.
value: Makes hub-and-spoke or mesh broker layouts observable from the canonical broker log stream without relying on ad-hoc grep during incidents.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to make hub-and-spoke or mesh broker layouts observable from the canonical broker log stream without relying on ad-hoc grep during incidents.
implementation: Normalize multi-line log wrapping. Build allowlists for planned maintenance connectors. Enrich with CMDB site codes via `host` lookup. For duplex=false links, separate inbound vs outbound patterns in follow-on searches.
visualization: Timeline of connector keywords, top connectors by event volume, geographic/site facet if enriched.
knownFalsePositives: Queues and broker metrics swing during rebalancing, replay, or maintenance. We align with change windows.
